header.title: Unit Converter — Length, Weight, Temp & More Online Free
header.description: Free online unit converter supporting length, weight, temperature, volume, area, speed, time, pressure, energy, and more measurement categories. Convert between metric and imperial units instantly.
header.keywords: unit converter, measurement converter, length converter, weight converter, temperature converter, metric to imperial, conversion calculator, units of measurement

howToUse.title: How to Use Unit Converter
howToUse.step1Desc: Choose a measurement category such as Length, Weight, Temperature, Volume, Area, Speed, Time, Pressure, or Energy.
howToUse.step2Desc: Select the unit you want to convert from and the unit you want to convert to.
howToUse.step3Desc: Type the measurement value in the input field. The converted result updates instantly.
howToUse.step4Desc: See the conversion formula used to calculate the result, helpful for learning how units relate.

items.question: What measurement categories are supported?
items.answer: The converter supports Length, Weight, Temperature, Volume, Area, Speed, Time, Pressure, Energy, Power, Force, and Data Storage. More categories can be added over time.

items.question: How accurate are the conversions?
items.answer: All conversions use standard international conversion factors with high-precision decimal arithmetic, ensuring results are accurate to at least 10 decimal places for most conversions.

items.question: Can I convert between metric and imperial units?
items.answer: Yes, every supported category includes both metric and imperial units. For example, you can convert kilometers to miles, kilograms to pounds, or Celsius to Fahrenheit.

items.question: How does temperature conversion work?
items.answer: Temperature conversions use the exact mathematical formulas: Celsius to Fahrenheit (°C × 9/5 + 32), Fahrenheit to Celsius ((°F − 32) × 5/9), and Kelvin conversions using the standard offset.

items.question: Can I see the formula used for each conversion?
items.answer: Yes, the conversion formula is displayed alongside the result. It shows the mathematical relationship between the two units, including any conversion factors and constants.

items.question: What should I do if a unit I need is missing?
items.answer: The unit list is regularly updated. If you need a specific unit that isn't listed, please provide feedback so it can be added in a future update.
